title: PostgreSQL Connection Refused in Docker
symptom: When trying to connect to a local PostgreSQL database from n8n running in Docker, the connection is refused.
applies_when: n8n is running in a Docker container
PostgreSQL is running on the host machine
not_this_if: PostgreSQL is also running in a Docker container
root_cause: The n8n instance running in a Docker container cannot access the PostgreSQL database on the host machine using localhost (127.0.0.1) because the container is isolated from the host network. Instead, it needs to use a special address to reach the host services.
fix_steps: Open the PostgreSQL node settings in n8n.
Save the changes and test the connection.
agent_instruction: Update the PostgreSQL node to use 'host.docker.internal' as the host address.
